title: build(deps): bump github.com/container-orchestrated-devices/container-device-interface from 0.3.0 to 0.3.1
body:
Bumps [github.com/container-orchestrated-devices/container-device-interface](https://github.com/container-orchestrated-devices/container-device-interface) from 0.3.0 to 0.3.1.
Release notes

Sourced from github.com/container-orchestrated-devices/container-device-interface's releases.

v0.3.1

What's Changed

  • go.{mod,sum}: relax selinux requirement to v1.9.1. by @​klihub in container-orchestrated-devices/container-device-interface#50
  • pkg/cdi: handle unmarshalling empty Spec data. by @​klihub in container-orchestrated-devices/container-device-interface#53
  • devel: perform schema-based Spec validation while loading Specs. by @​klihub in container-orchestrated-devices/container-device-interface#54

Full Changelog: https://github.com/container-orchestrated-devices/container-device-interface/compare/v0.3.0...v0.3.1

Commits
  • 4b00593 Merge pull request #54 from klihub/devel/schema-based-validation
  • 2325a1f cmd/cdi: add validate --schema command line flag.
  • b60ae60 schema/testdata: fix spec-example.json.
  • c0729c6 schema: add/update schema validation tests.
  • b3e367e cmd/validate: refactor/simplify validator code.
  • 711b138 pkg/cdi,schema: add schema-based Spec validation.
  • 4adc5b7 schema,cmd/validate: move validate to {cmd,bin}.
  • 4d9b881 Merge pull request #53 from klihub/fixes/handle-empty-spec-gracefully
  • 1b56e88 pkg/cdi: handle unmarshal setting pointer to nil.
  • e545712 Merge pull request #50 from klihub/fixes/relax-selinux-dependency
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

You can also trigger a rebase manually by commenting `@dependabot rebase`. [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-start) [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-end) ---
Dependabot commands and options
You can trigger Dependabot actions by commenting on this PR: - `@dependabot rebase` will rebase this PR - `@dependabot recreate` will recreate this PR, overwriting any edits that have been made to it - `@dependabot merge` will merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot squash and merge` will squash and merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot cancel merge` will cancel a previously requested merge and block automerging - `@dependabot reopen` will reopen this PR if it is closed - `@dependabot close` will close this PR and stop Dependabot recreating it. You can achieve the same result by closing it manually - `@dependabot ignore this major version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this major version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this minor version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this minor version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this dependency` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this dependency (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself)

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *dependabot[bot]* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **giuseppe** after the PR has been reviewed. You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @giuseppe` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Test failures also look legitimate though

I don't have enough knowledge to know what the cdi library does or what we actually use.

Looking at the diff here I can see they added quite a bit of logic into init functions, this will cause podman startup to slow down. I don't see why we they have to validate the spec files add init time. Most of our code path will definitely never need this.
It also looks like they embed the json spec directly into the binary so this will also cause a size increase.
I browsed the code more and I think we should definitely try to fix this upstream, we use just a tiny bit of their code. This size increase is unacceptable for what podman is actually using IMO.
Could you open a PR upstream?
I will have a look at what's causing this in CDI. cc @klihub
We have a fix in progress as https://github.com/container-orchestrated-devices/container-device-interface/pull/62 and will bump our version once that has merged.
Looks like it was merged, now do we need to wait for a new release or should we just vendor in that commit?

> Test failures also look legitimate though

The CDI test failures were due to an incorrect mount options type in the built-in verification schema. There's a PR with a fix pending for that. Also, since we don't try half-accidentally forcing validation down everybody's throat anymore, that should not bite the test cases in the heinie either...

> Looks like it was merged, now do we need to wait for a new release or should we just vendor in that commit?

If we were to push a `v0.3.2` tag would we repurpose this PR for that update too?

> > Looks like it was merged, now do we need to wait for a new release or should we just vendor in that commit?
> 
> If we were to push a `v0.3.2` tag would we repurpose this PR for that update too?

I think dependabot creates a new PR and closes this one.

> > > Looks like it was merged, now do we need to wait for a new release or should we just vendor in that commit?
> > 
> > 
> > If we were to push a `v0.3.2` tag would we repurpose this PR for that update too?
> 
> I think dependabot creates a new PR and closes this one.

OK. I went ahead and pushed the tag and also created #13852 to perform the update.
OK I close this one then

OK, I won't notify you again about this release, but will get in touch when a new version is available. If you'd rather skip all updates until the next major or minor version, let me know by commenting `@dependabot ignore this major version` or `@dependabot ignore this minor version`. You can also ignore all major, minor, or patch releases for a dependency by adding an [`ignore` condition](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/supply-chain-security/configuration-options-for-dependency-updates#ignore) with the desired `update_types` to your config file.

If you change your mind, just re-open this PR and I'll resolve any conflicts on it.
